title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I am very excited. It is 11 a.m. now and I'm quite hungry for breakfast, so I'm going to go to a restaurant and try a Korean food that I have never had before.
I need to walk a little bit, so I am heading to the meal flea market. I love antiques and vintage shopping. I've never been there before, so I'm going to hop on a bus to go to Dongmyo.

I decided to come back home for a little while. I actually killed two birds with one stone because I shopped at the market, which was on my bucket list, and I also bought some yogurts from the yogur lady. This has been something I've wanted to do in Korea for a very long time, but for some reason, whenever I see the yogurts ladies driving their carts on the street, I get really nervous and shy, but today I faced my fears. So I got this one, which is classic, and I do know this one, but I've never tried it. And I also asked for another recommendation, and she gave me this wheel, so something full of probiotics. That's good for digestion. So I am going to try both of these in a minute, but first, let me review the market a little bit. So usually I like having a partner in crime when I go to places like that because I am so indecisive, but I did end up buying some sunglasses. I'll try them on. What do we think guys? As I walked around Dongmyo market, it actually reminded me of something similar we have back home. So in the US, we do have flea markets here and there, but where I'm from in the summertime, we also do something called garage sales. It's very common for neighborhoods to have a garage sale weekend, where every house in the neighborhood will open up their garages and set things outside for people to come walk around and shop and buy some secondhand goods. I feel like in markets like Dongmyo, you really have to dig to find something good. So we have a saying, one man's trash is another man's treasure, and before I went to this market, I heard some people say, oh, it's just full of junk. But I heard other people say that it's a gem and you must visit Dongmyo. So I had no clue what to expect, but I did enjoy it. I like that atmosphere a lot. I feel like I'm stepping back in time.

Dang, this is so delicious. Oh my gosh, I love both of these. I'm kind of sad that I did not do this sooner. What a gem the yogurt ladies are. Oh, I love it. So anyways, the first half of my bucket list day is done. And now I am heading out to meet up with my friend Doho. We have a few more fun things planned. So let's go. I am doing Saju. So I am going to a fortune teller to see if I have a good fortune if I'll be lucky this upcoming year. If they say I have bad luck or something bad is going to happen to me, I think I'll be a little bit paranoid. So fingers crossed for good news from the fortune teller tonight. I just finished my Saju and it was a very interesting reading.

Definitely not what I expected at all. She basically said that I'm going to have a lot of young men around me when I'm older. So I'm going to be a cougar when I'm older and she said I'll have a lot of money. So that's good. And she said that my husband and I should do business together. And I think that was the best news since that's what we're doing already. Overall I would say it was a pretty positive reading. Although I enjoy going to fortune tellers, I always take what they say with a grain of salt. So I think that I am in control of my own destiny. Anyways, now we are going out for dinner. I have never tried duck galbi before and I've heard it is bomb. All right, let's go eat some food. My god, that's so much cheese. Let's try it with the radish.

title: will / shall
examples: I'll be there for three months, so I was thinking about things I haven't done yet in Korea that I really want to do before I leave.
So good. Super tangy and fresh. First bite ever of this dish. I'll try the broth first.
I think the kimchi will complement this very well since the kimchi is quite spicy and has a bold flavor. It will go perfect with this soup.
I will definitely order it again in the future. So now, after eating a meal, I'm very full.

I have made it to Dongmyo flea market. The first thing I did was pick up some coffee because I am a little bit sleepy after such a big meal for breakfast. I don't really have anything in particular that I want to buy at the markets today, so I'm just going to kind of browse. But if I happen to find some nice sunglasses, maybe I'll buy a pair because recently my sunglasses broke or since winter is coming, maybe a nice sweater or hoodie, something like that. So I'll keep my eyes out for those items. Otherwise, I'm just going to enjoy the environment of the markets.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: I will be making a lot more content like this. So please make sure to subscribe to my channel and I'll see you next time. Bye!

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: I'll be there for three months, so I was thinking about things I haven't done yet in Korea that I really want to do before I leave.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: You can see the steam from the soup. I always like to start with the side dishes.
I think the dressing is so good because I can really taste the sesame oil and I love the flavor of sesame. These pieces of radish are so big. Oh my gosh. I'm excited for the crunch.

I watched Netflix while I was waiting for my game to download and that was about it. My friend also watched Netflix because we had no clue what we were doing. But it looked really fun when I saw other people gaming. So I really want to try again. If you have any game recommendations, please let me know. Even though that part of the day was kind of a fail, my friend and I were laughing so hard about it and it was a great memory. The whole day was a lot of fun and I'm glad I could finally check some things off of my Korean bucket list. I hope you enjoyed spending the day with me.
